gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A declared byte-read budget (C6/RFC-0014 §4.5; ADR-015)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Passed to [`read`] to cap the allocation.  An overrun yields"] # [doc = " [`IoError::EffectBudget`] instead of unbounded allocation (C6)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `Unbounded` variant is available for in-memory tests where an allocation"] # [doc = " ceiling is not meaningful; production uses of the OS-backed substrate should"] # [doc = " always supply `Bytes(n)` (spec §7-Q5 / RFC-0016 §8-Q3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Budget { # [doc = " Read at most `n` bytes; any request beyond this yields"] # [doc = " [`IoError::EffectBudget`]."] Bytes (u64) , # [doc = " No cap — accept any number of bytes.  Use only in tests or when the"] # [doc = " caller has an out-of-band cap (e.g. the file system size)."] Unbounded , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The in-memory substrate: a `Vec<u8>` cursor."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `substrate` is the **underlying resource** that `Source`/`Sink` wrap.  A"] # [doc = " [`Source`] owns a `Substrate` by-value; once consumed (by [`read_all`] or the"] # [doc = " last [`read`]) it cannot be re-accessed.  This is the LR-8 affine invariant at"] # [doc = " the type level."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG: §8-Q6 — OS-backed substrate lives in `std-sys` (M-541)"] # [doc = " The real-OS-backed substrate (a file descriptor, a network socket) is NOT"] # [doc = " here.  The `std-sys` phylum will provide a constructor like"] # [doc = " `Substrate::from_fd(fd: OwnedFd) -> Substrate` that slots into this type."] # [doc = " Until then this in-memory substrate covers all testable semantics."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct Substrate { # [doc = " The raw bytes (in-memory backing store)."] data : Vec < u8 > , # [doc = " Read cursor position."] pos : usize , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An abstract byte **source**: a `Substrate` wrapped in an affine handle."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A `Source` may be read exactly once via [`read_all`] (which consumes it) or"] # [doc = " progressively via [`read`] (which threads the handle linearly).  A double-"] # [doc = " consume is a compile-time error (Rust move semantics enforce LR-8)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN-able selection"] # [doc = " The substrate backing this `Source` is visible as a field (C3 — no hidden"] # [doc = " resource)."] # [derive (Debug)] pub struct Source { substrate : Substrate , }

gaps.snippet: impl Source { # [doc = " Wrap a substrate as an affine `Source`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: none (construction is pure)"] # [must_use] pub fn new (substrate : Substrate) -> Self { Source { substrate } } # [doc = " Construct a `Source` directly from an in-memory byte slice (convenience"] # [doc = " constructor for tests)."] # [must_use] pub fn from_bytes (data : impl Into < Vec < u8 > >) -> Self { Source :: new (Substrate :: from_bytes (data)) } # [doc = " How many bytes remain unread."] # [must_use] pub fn remaining (& self) -> usize { self . substrate . remaining () } # [doc = " Read up to `n` bytes from the substrate (internal, non-consuming helper)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns the bytes read and the new cursor position."] fn read_n (& mut self , n : usize) -> Vec < u8 > { let end = (self . substrate . pos + n) . min (self . substrate . data . len ()) ; let chunk = self . substrate . data [self . substrate . pos .. end] . to_vec () ; self . substrate . pos = end ; chunk } # [doc = " Read all remaining bytes (internal, consuming helper)."] fn read_to_end (& mut self) -> Vec < u8 > { let bytes = self . substrate . data [self . substrate . pos ..] . to_vec () ; self . substrate . pos = self . substrate . data . len () ; bytes } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An abstract byte **sink**: a write target wrapped in an affine handle."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A `Sink` is threaded linearly through [`write()`] calls (each call consumes the"] # [doc = " current handle and returns the updated one). gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Read up to `budget` bytes from `src`, returning the bytes and the remaining"] # [doc = " handle (LR-8 — linear threading)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The caller receives the `Source` back so it may continue reading.  A call that"] # [doc = " returns an empty `Vec` has exhausted the source; subsequent calls will also"] # [doc = " return empty (they never error on a naturally-exhausted source)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " The bytes returned are exactly the bytes the substrate delivers up to the"] # [doc = " budget."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " - `Err(IoError::EffectBudget{kind: \"alloc\"})` if `budget` would require more"] # [doc = "   allocation than the declared `alloc(Budget)` policy (for the in-memory"] # [doc = "   substrate, `Unbounded` never triggers this)."] # [doc = " - `Err(IoError::Refused{why})` if the substrate declines."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: io + alloc(Budget) (declared, C6)"] pub fn read (src : Source , budget : Budget) -> Result < (Vec < u8 > , Source) , IoError > { let mut src = src ; let n = match budget { Budget :: Bytes (cap) => { (cap as usize) . min (src . remaining ()) } Budget :: Unbounded => src . remaining () , } ; let chunk = src . read_n (n) ; Ok ((chunk , src)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Write `bytes` to `snk`, consuming the handle and returning the updated one"] # [doc = " (LR-8 — linear threading)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact`"] # [doc = " The bytes written are exactly `bytes` — neither reordered nor approximated."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " - `Err(IoError::Refused{why})` if the substrate declines."] # [doc = " - `Err(IoError::EffectBudget{kind})` on an effect-budget overrun."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: io (declared, C6)"] pub fn write (snk : Sink , bytes : & [u8]) -> Result < Sink , IoError > { let mut snk = snk ; snk . append (bytes) ; Ok (snk) }

# [doc = " Deserialize a `Value` directly from `src` in the given `format`, joining the"] # [doc = " io and serialize halves."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Empirical` (round-trip; composes io + deserialize; spec §4)"] # [doc = " The tag is the meet of `Exact` (io) and `Empirical` (deserialize) = `Empirical`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Fallibility"] # [doc = " `Err(ReadValueError::Io(_))` — byte-movement failure before/during decode."] # [doc = " `Err(ReadValueError::Ser(_))` — bytes read but decode failed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Effects: io (declared, C6)"] pub fn read_value (src : Source , format : crate :: serialize :: Format ,) -> Result < mycelium_core :: Value , crate :: error :: ReadValueError > { let bytes = read_all (src) . map_err (crate :: error :: ReadValueError :: Io) ? ; crate :: serialize :: deserialize (& bytes , format) . map_err (crate :: error :: ReadValueError :: Ser) }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; use crate :: error :: ByteCount ; # [test] fn substrate_remaining_starts_full () { let s = Substrate :: from_bytes (vec ! [1u8 , 2 , 3 , 4]) ; assert_eq ! (s . remaining () , 4) ; } # [doc = " `read_all` returns all bytes from the source."] # [doc = " Guard: returning fewer bytes makes this fail."] # [test] fn read_all_returns_all_bytes () { let data = vec ! [10u8 , 20 , 30 , 40 , 50] ; let src = Source :: from_bytes (data . clone ()) ; let bytes = read_all (src) . expect ("read_all must succeed") ; assert_eq ! (bytes , data , "read_all must return exactly the source bytes") ; } # [doc = " `read_all` consumes the source exactly once — the handle is moved."] # [doc = " This is a compile-time property (Rust move semantics), but we test the"] # [doc = " observable behavior: calling read_all twice on the *same data* via"] # [doc = " separate Sources both succeed independently."] # [doc = " Guard: a double-consume bug (reuse without a new Source) would be a"] # [doc = " compile error, not reachable here."] # [test] fn read_all_consumed_once_semantics () { let data = vec ! [1u8 , 2 , 3] ; let src1 = Source :: from_bytes (data . clone ()) ; let src2 = Source :: from_bytes (data . clone ()) ; let r1 = read_all (src1) . expect ("first source") ; let r2 = read_all (src2) . expect ("second source") ; assert_eq ! (r1 , r2 , "two separate sources of same data return same bytes") ; } # [doc = " `read_all` on an empty source returns an empty Vec (not an error)."] # [test] fn read_all_empty_source_returns_empty () { let src = Source :: from_bytes (vec ! []) ; let bytes = read_all (src) . expect ("empty source must succeed") ; assert ! (bytes . is_empty () , "empty source must yield empty bytes") ; } # [doc = " `read` with `Budget::Unbounded` returns all remaining bytes."] # [test] fn read_unbounded_returns_all () { let data = vec ! [1u8 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5] ; let src = Source :: from_bytes (data . clone ()) ; let (chunk , src_rest) = read (src , Budget :: Unbounded) . expect ("read must succeed") ; assert_eq ! (chunk , data) ; assert_eq ! (src_rest . remaining () , 0 , "source must be exhausted") ; } # [doc = " `read` with `Budget::Bytes(n)` reads at most `n` bytes."] # [doc = " Guard: reading more than the budget makes this fail."] # [test] fn read_bounded_reads_at_most_budget () { let data = vec ! [1u8 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5] ; let src = Source :: from_bytes (data . clone ()) ; let (chunk , _src_rest) = read (src , Budget :: Bytes (3)) . expect ("read must succeed") ; assert_eq ! (chunk , vec ! [1u8 , 2 , 3] , "must read exactly 3 bytes") ; } # [doc = " `read` returns the handle for continuation — linear threading."] # [doc = " Two consecutive reads cover all bytes."] # [test] fn read_linear_continuation () { let data = vec ! [10u8 , 20 , 30 , 40] ; let src = Source :: from_bytes (data . clone ()) ; let (chunk1 , src) = read (src , Budget :: Bytes (2)) . expect ("first read") ; let (chunk2 , src) = read (src , Budget :: Bytes (2)) . expect ("second read") ; assert_eq ! (chunk1 , vec ! [10u8 , 20]) ; assert_eq ! (chunk2 , vec ! [30u8 , 40]) ; assert_eq ! (src . remaining () , 0 , "fully consumed after two reads") ; } # [doc = " `read` when budget exceeds remaining returns only what's available (no error)."] # [test] fn read_budget_exceeds_remaining_returns_partial () { let data = vec ! [1u8 , 2] ; let src = Source :: from_bytes (data . clone ()) ; let (chunk , src_rest) = read (src , Budget :: Bytes (100)) . expect ("read past end") ; assert_eq ! (chunk , data , "must return what's available, not 100 bytes") ; assert_eq ! (src_rest . remaining () , 0) ; } # [doc = " `write` appends bytes to the sink and returns the updated handle."] # [test] fn write_appends_bytes () { let snk = Sink :: new () ; let snk = write (snk , & [1u8 , 2 , 3]) . expect ("first write") ; let snk = write (snk , & [4u8 , 5]) . expect ("second write") ; let result = snk . into_bytes () ; assert_eq ! (result , vec ! [1u8 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5] , "bytes must be appended in order") ; } # [doc = " `write` with empty bytes is a no-op (no error)."] # [test] fn write_empty_bytes_is_noop () { let snk = Sink :: new () ; let snk = write (snk , & []) . expect ("write empty") ; assert ! (snk . into_bytes () . is_empty ()) ; } # [doc = " The bytes retrieved by `read_all` are byte-for-byte equal to the bytes"] # [doc = " given to `Source::from_bytes` — exactness of the byte-movement guarantee."] # [doc = " Guard: any transformation inside read_all makes this fail."] # [test] fn read_all_is_exact () { let data : Vec < u8 > = (0u8 ..= 255) . collect () ; let src = Source :: from_bytes (data . clone ()) ; let result = read_all (src) . expect ("read_all") ; assert_eq ! (result , data , "byte-movement must be exact (spec §4 / Exact tag)") ; } # [doc = " The bytes retrieved by `write` + `into_bytes` are byte-for-byte equal to"] # [doc = " the bytes given to `write`."] # [test] fn write_is_exact () { let data : Vec < u8 > = (0u8 ..= 255) . collect () ; let snk = Sink :: new () ; let snk = write (snk , & data) . expect ("write") ; let result = snk . into_bytes () ; assert_eq ! (result , data , "byte-movement must be exact (spec §4 / Exact tag)") ; } # [doc = " `IoError::UnexpectedEof` carries the byte count (RFC-0013 I1 / C1)."] # [doc = " Guard: dropping the count from the error makes this fail."] # [test] fn io_error_unexpected_eof_carries_count () { let e = IoError :: UnexpectedEof { read : ByteCount (7) } ; assert ! (e . to_string () . contains ("7") , "error must carry the byte count (RFC-0013 I1)") ; } # [doc = " `read_value` round-trips a serialized value end-to-end."] # [doc = " Guard: any divergence between serialize → source → read_value makes this fail."] # [test] fn read_value_round_trip () { use crate :: serialize :: { serialize , Format } ; use mycelium_core :: { meta :: { Meta , Provenance } , repr :: Repr , value :: { Payload , Value } , } ; let v = Value :: new (Repr :: Binary { width : 4 } , Payload :: Bits (vec ! (v , recovered , "read_value must recover the original value") ; } # [doc = " `read_value` on garbage bytes yields `Err(ReadValueError::Ser(_))` (C1)."] # [test] fn read_value_garbage_bytes_yields_ser_error () { use crate :: serialize :: Format ; let src = Source :: from_bytes (b"not valid json at all" . to_vec ()) ; let err = read_value (src , Format :: Wire) . expect_err ("must error on garbage") ; assert !
